109325.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that local government reflects Arnhem well. Therefore, we strive for an equal number of men and women in the executive board.

109326.explanation: In Arnhem, residents have had the option to request advisory referendums for years.

109329.explanation: To counter nuisance in the (inner) city, cafes and restaurants cannot always remain open. For example, certain areas like the Korenmarkt may permit nighttime opening, but this is not always appropriate in residential neighborhoods.

109331.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must return to their country of origin when the asylum application is definitively rejected. As long as return is not successful, we provide shelter so they do not end up on the street.

109333.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves emit many harmful substances, which particularly affect people with lung complaints.

109335.explanation: In recent years, experiments have been done with fireworks and light shows. We believe that New Year's Eve should be pleasant for everyone. This can be achieved by the municipality organizing an activity for the city. Various forms are possible.

109337.explanation: Arnhem is a lively city where people live, work, and engage in activities. Events are part of this. To ensure events are enjoyable for visitors and limit nuisance for residents, we want to consider more than just noise standards.

109338.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

109342.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

109343.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

109345.explanation: In recent years, the number of enforcement officers in Arnhem has been significantly increased. Much effort is also put into prevention, for example with youth workers. We want to continue this approach.

109346.explanation: The number of enforcement officers has increased and preventive efforts, such as youth workers, have expanded. Within privacy rules, the mayor can apply (temporary) camera surveillance.

109348.explanation: In Arnhem, we want to build many more affordable and good houses. A starter loan only helps to buy even more expensive houses and take on greater financial burdens. Building affordable homes is better for people than higher monthly costs.

109349.explanation: The housing allocation system in Arnhem applies to the entire region. Within that system, we believe residents from our region should have priority for social rental housing.

109354.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

109356.explanation: We want to support more people with financial arrangements so they can make ends meet better and have fewer financial worries. This enables them to participate in city life.

109358.explanation: We support a trial with a basic income in Arnhem to see how this works for people.

109361.explanation: There is already paid parking in the inner city and many houses are being added near large shopping centers where paid parking is introduced. Neighborhood residents receive an inexpensive or free permit that they can also use at the local shopping center.

109363.explanation: People deserve care that suits them.

109367.explanation: The revenue from tourist tax contributes to the work the municipality does in the interest of the city.

109368.explanation: Space in Arnhem is limited and thousands of homes will be added in coming years. The space for certain industries is decreasing, while other types of work will have more room. This will mostly be combined with housing and less on separate industrial estates.

109370.explanation: We want refugees allowed to stay in the Netherlands to be able to participate in the city from day one. Without housing, they remain long in (crowded) shelters. This is harmful, especially for growing children. Therefore, they receive urgency status, just like many other housing seekers.

109371.explanation: Currently, it is possible to bring waste to the recycling center free of charge six times a year. We want to maintain this possibility.

109375.explanation: If a new coffeeshop wants to establish in Arnhem, we are open to locations where this fits best, for example regarding space and nuisance prevention.

109379.explanation: Every neighborhood deserves a community center where people can meet and organize activities.

109381.explanation: We want much less street parking and more use of parking in garages. This creates more space on the streets for, for example, greenery, play areas, or activities.

109383.explanation: The waiting time for a social rental home in Arnhem is over 18 years. Therefore, we want to build many more social rental homes in Arnhem.

109384.explanation: Many people love Arnhem because of our parks and nature. If we start building on these areas, we lose them forever. We do not want to do that because Arnhem must be a city where it is pleasant to live.

109385.explanation: To improve air quality in the busy city center, especially for people with lung conditions, we want to restrict a part of the center to the oldest petrol and diesel vans of companies. They will receive support to make the switch or can use an alternative route.

109325.explanation: It is important that the executive board represents the city well, including in terms of gender. We strongly consider this when nominating candidates, especially those we nominate ourselves. A mandatory equal distribution is too rigid.

109326.explanation: We want to involve residents at all stages of decision-making. At the start through participation and new forms of consultation such as citizens' assemblies, and at the end through the corrective referendum that Arnhem already has.

109329.explanation: Arnhem has everything needed to be and remain a lively city. This depends on initiatives – big and small – by Arnhemmers who want to create something fun. The municipality should get out of their way as much as possible with rules and obstacles.

109331.explanation: People fleeing war and violence always have a place in Arnhem. However, a good asylum system requires quick clarity, both for applicants and the municipality. When asylum is definitively rejected, the municipality no longer offers shelter and supports people in their return to their home country.

109333.explanation: We grant everyone the freedom to light a fire, but that freedom conflicts with the restriction of thousands of Arnhemmers suffering from smoke, sometimes so severely they cannot go outside. Therefore, we want to gradually reduce wood burning.

109335.explanation: New Year's Eve should be a celebration for everyone. A joint municipal show can contribute to that. However, this does not have to be a fireworks display but could also be with drones or a light show. That is much more sustainable and less harmful to nature.

109337.explanation: A vibrant Arnhem stands or falls with initiatives – big and small – by Arnhemmers who want to organize something enjoyable. Sometimes this involves a bit of nuisance.

109338.explanation: Everyone who wants to participate in Arnhem must be given the chance. The presence of barriers (literally) should not be an obstacle. Initially, this responsibility lies with employers themselves, but where bottlenecks occur, the municipality should step in.

109342.explanation: We ensure that all children have the opportunity to learn to play an instrument. We do this with active support and cooperation between schools, music schools, clubs, and other providers.

109343.explanation: Sporting is extremely important for staying healthy, good for social cohesion, and also simply fun. It is in the interest of everyone in the city that it remains accessible to all Arnhemmers.

109345.explanation: More enforcement does not automatically mean more safety. Our priority is preventing and addressing the underlying causes of crime. Therefore, we prefer more neighborhood police officers and detectives who can tackle (the financial flows of) organized crime.

109346.explanation: Cameras can improve the feeling of safety but also infringe on privacy. Currently, we mainly have many private cameras in Arnhem. The priority should be better regulation and utilization of these cameras before installing more.

109348.explanation: Many Arnhemmers search in vain for a home. Therefore, we want to build thousands of additional homes on top of existing plans. Offering starter loans does not reduce the shortage but only drives up house prices further.

109349.explanation: Many Arnhemmers search in vain for a home. Many look beyond municipal borders, for example, to Duiven or Velp. Therefore, we make agreements with neighboring municipalities not to create barriers at the municipal border.

109354.explanation: Social assistance is a transition, not an end station. We mainly want to help people who rely on it to get back to work. This can be by requesting a counter-performance. This does not necessarily have to be work but can also be fulfilled by following education.

109356.explanation: Currently, Arnhem has a jumble of schemes, many of which are not used by even half of the people they are meant for. This needs to be simpler and more targeted. The municipality must provide much more proactive customized support to Arnhemmers who need it.

109358.explanation: A radical simplification of our benefits system is a good idea. However, the central government is primarily responsible for this. We also do what we can. To guarantee subsistence security and ensure that working pays off, we optimize municipal income schemes.

109361.explanation: Parking spaces take up a lot of space. Space that then cannot be used for housing, recreation, or greenery. It is therefore fair to charge users of this space accordingly.

109363.explanation: Free choice of care is important for people's sense of freedom. Therefore, we maintain diversity in offerings among different providers and leave the choice as much as possible to the people themselves.

109367.explanation: We want Arnhem to remain an attractive city for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. We use the income from tourist tax to invest in that attractiveness, for example, in the city center, better promotion, and good connections within the city.

109368.explanation: We want to build a huge number of homes, and space in Arnhem is limited. This will therefore come at the expense of industrial areas. To keep our city economically strong, we invest heavily in better organizing these areas and look at new locations, although options are limited.

109370.explanation: People fleeing war and violence must always find a place in Arnhem. To ensure that they can participate and integrate in Arnhem from day one, quick housing is necessary.

109371.explanation: Whoever litters must pay the costs of cleaning up themselves. It is not fair if someone else has to pay for your waste.

109375.explanation: D66 advocates for a realistic drug policy where we regulate and discourage drugs like cannabis and ecstasy strictly, just like alcohol and tobacco, but do not make their production and trade illegal. We distribute coffee shops better across the city, including a new coffee shop in the South.

109379.explanation: Socially strong neighborhoods are the main condition for a livable city. As a municipality, we must invest in them and give them space.

109381.explanation: Parking spaces take up a lot of space. Space that then cannot be used for housing, recreation, or greenery. With a park-and-ride location outside the center with good connections, we ensure the center remains accessible to visitors.

109383.explanation: Currently, social rental housing is unevenly distributed across Arnhem. This fosters unequal opportunities. Therefore, we want to better spread it across the city. In neighborhoods where more than 50 percent of the housing is social rental, no additional social rental will be added.

109384.explanation: Many Arnhemmers search in vain for a home. Therefore, we want to build thousands of additional homes on top of existing plans. We look first at building within the existing city but do not exclude building in green areas outright.

109385.explanation: We want you to breathe clean air in Arnhem, especially where we come together to meet and enjoy.

109325.explanation: You should strive for the best possible diversity in the executive board and council, but quality comes first, so no quotas.

109326.explanation: We are in favor of more pre-consultation and therefore against referendums afterward.

109329.explanation: Agreed, unless safety and livability are seriously at risk.

109331.explanation: We do want to offer support and shelter to rejected asylum seekers who cooperate with their return. This means that for the group who does not cooperate and has received a court rejection, there is no shelter in Arnhem.

109335.explanation: If there is an initiative from the city, the municipality must facilitate it.

109337.explanation: The noise standards in Arnhem are already strict. You must enforce them; otherwise, it is pointless.

109338.explanation: We believe it is important that everyone can participate. And if entrepreneurs need help with that, we believe we should assist them.

109342.explanation: This is wonderful, but not a municipal responsibility.

109343.explanation: Sports are important, sports clubs even more so. In our view, occasional emergency aid is acceptable, but no structural support.

109345.explanation: According to the mayor, we currently need at least 35 more enforcement officers to reach a total of 70. That is what cities comparable to Arnhem require.

109346.explanation: It has been proven in Arnhem that camera surveillance can contribute to a safer city, so we must also provide space for it. This means that alongside camera surveillance, we will continue to explore other options to implement measures that contribute to a safe city.

109348.explanation: We agree with the starter loan, but this is not a municipal task. This should be handled by the national government.

109349.explanation: If each municipality mainly does this, we ensure that the correct number of homes can be built fairly for those who need them. In this way, we also contribute to preserving the social contribution of these Arnhem residents to the city.

109354.explanation: In Arnhem, we take care of each other, but always based on the principle of reciprocity: those who receive help do what they can to give something back.

109356.explanation: VVD Arnhem stands for a strong safety net for those who truly need it, close to home and of high quality. With clear agreements, fair distribution, and attention to the human dimension, we are building a city where everyone can participate and shape their own future.

109358.explanation: VVD Arnhem stands for a strong safety net for those who truly need it, close to home and of high quality. With clear agreements, fair distribution, and attention to the human dimension, we are building a city where everyone can participate and shape their own future.

109361.explanation: We cannot deny or forget car ownership in our urban development. We must also make our mobility future-proof and dare to invest in it. That does not mean immediately limiting or banning it, but first ensuring good, attractive alternatives.

109363.explanation: What does this have to do with the municipality?

109367.explanation: The revenues must benefit the sector itself.

109368.explanation: Arnhem must focus on the city's earning capacity, with attention to large companies that need space, such as TenneT. Arnhem businesses and institutions must feel welcome and valued.

109370.explanation: Newcomers do not have priority over established Arnhem residents. This means priority is not a right, but an option.

109371.explanation: A clean, well-maintained city is good for livability and for pride in Arnhem.

109375.explanation: VVD Arnhem supports a phase-out policy.

109379.explanation: If necessary and there is support in the neighborhood, it should be allowed.

109381.explanation: We cannot deny or forget car ownership in our urban development. We must also make our mobility future-proof and dare to invest in it. That does not mean immediately limiting or banning it, but first ensuring good, attractive alternatives.

109383.explanation: As far as VVD Arnhem is concerned, we build what is needed. No dogmas. We set forth what kind of city we want to be in twenty years, who we want and can facilitate, what the housing desires and possibilities are. A logical and feasible mix.

109384.explanation: For VVD Arnhem, municipal space is primarily meant for living and working, and green space is intended for relaxation, recreation, and sports.

109385.explanation: We cannot deny or forget car ownership in our urban development. We must also make our mobility future-proof and dare to invest in it. That does not mean immediately limiting or banning it, but first ensuring good, attractive alternatives.

109325.explanation: Arnhem Centraal strives for a balanced gender distribution in the council. Not as a strict requirement, but as an ambition. Knowledge, experience, and administrative quality come first; diversity strengthens decision-making and makes governance recognizable to all Arnhem residents.

109326.explanation: Residents should have more influence over major decisions. Referendums can, if used carefully, contribute to support and involvement in important decisions.

109329.explanation: Arnhem Centraal considers fixed closing times necessary to limit nuisance for residents and to relieve police and enforcement officers. On Fridays and Saturdays until 3:00 a.m., other days until 2:00 a.m. This provides clarity and allows scarce enforcement resources to be used more effectively.

109331.explanation: No one should end up on the streets. Shelter for rejected asylum seekers remains necessary, but not indefinitely. This requires clear agreements with other authorities, strict supervision, and consistent guidance to prevent nuisance and maintain support.

109333.explanation: Wood smoke causes nuisance and daily life restrictions, but a total ban is not realistic. Arnhem Centraal wants clear rules during smog or heat, stricter enforcement against nuisance, and first insight into who depends on wood burning/usage, such as scouts or households.

109335.explanation: Residents of Arnhem deserve a full-fledged year-end event. Fireworks shows, near the John Frost Bridge or spread throughout the city, are safer, more unifying, and reduce nuisance.

109337.explanation: Events belong to Arnhem, but livability comes first. Clear standards per event and proper enforcement are more important than simply making everything stricter.

109338.explanation: Buildings must be accessible to everyone. Entrepreneurs are obliged to make adjustments, but the municipality can support with knowledge and advice. This way, Arnhem truly becomes a city without obstacles.

109342.explanation: In Arnhem, every child must have the chance to discover talent and develop themselves, whether in sports or in the music hall. Both sports and culture deserve support, otherwise we lose young talents.

109343.explanation: Sports clubs are the heart of Arnhem's neighborhoods: they bring people together and keep us healthy. Arnhem Centraal believes the municipality should step in when clubs are struggling, because without them the city loses its strength. The condition is that management fulfills its duties responsibly.

109345.explanation: Every Arnhem resident must feel safe in their neighborhood. Arnhem Centraal recognizes the great need and therefore wants to invest in well-trained, visible enforcement officers. In this way, we safeguard livability, peace, and trust, with respect for the residents.

109346.explanation: The need is great: targeted expansion of camera surveillance prevents problems and enables faster intervention. Arnhem Centraal opts for deployment in the right locations, combined with other safety measures such as improved lighting.

109348.explanation: Young Arnhem residents deserve more opportunities in the housing market. A starter loan can help, but the financial risks must be carefully assessed. It should make housing accessible without further driving up prices.

109349.explanation: Priority only for Arnhem residents sounds fair but does not solve the housing shortage. Arnhem Centraal opts for a transparent and fair system in which urgency, need, and flow are leading, not your postcode.

109354.explanation: People on welfare should gradually be given perspective. Mandatory 'reciprocity' often stigmatizes and does not help. We choose guidance to suitable (volunteer) work, with respect for the personal situation. This way we strengthen their social network and societal chances.

109356.explanation: Arnhem Centraal wants support to be accessible to everyone who needs it. Even people who just fall outside current regulations should have space. We carefully consider affordability, but choose security of existence and prevent residents from falling through the cracks.

109358.explanation: A basic income sounds interesting, but there is still too little research to implement it responsibly. Without support from the national government, it can be financially challenging. Arnhem Centraal sees it as a possible experiment in the future, but for now still too uncertain to pursue.

109361.explanation: Arnhem Centraal wants essential facilities such as the Presikhaaf and Kronenburg shopping centers to remain easily accessible for residents who need a car. Where residents experience nuisance, we look for appropriate measures. We seek a balance between accessibility and livability.

109363.explanation: Arnhem believes care should be as much as possible tailored to norms, culture, or religion, but full freedom of choice is organizationally unfeasible. Preferences can be considered but are not guaranteed; accessibility and feasibility come first.

109367.explanation: Arnhem must remain attractive to visitors and fair to residents. Tourist tax should be balanced with what the city offers and what services are needed. We see no reason to lower it.

109368.explanation: Arnhem Centraal does not choose large-scale expansion of business parks but focuses on better utilization and sustainability of existing locations. Expansion is only possible carefully and in balance with the living environment, nature, and the scarce space for housing.

109370.explanation: Arnhem is legally obligated to accommodate status holders. Arnhem Centraal believes that refugees with a residence permit should quickly receive housing so they can participate and prevent the shelter system from becoming overloaded. This concerns a small portion of social rental housing.

109371.explanation: Residents of Arnhem can already bring waste six times a year for free. That is sufficient. Additional visits incur (smaller) costs that contribute to lower waste disposal fees for everyone. Making it completely free offers no advantage and actually increases the burden.

109375.explanation: Arnhem has enough coffeeshops. New locations bring more risks than benefits, especially regarding safety and livability. Arnhem Centraal chooses to properly manage the existing shops and focus on them.

109379.explanation: Every neighborhood deserves a place to come together, but that does not have to be a new community center. Arnhem Centraal opts for existing facilities or nearby locations, mostly run by volunteers, to preserve social interaction and livability.

109381.explanation: Arnhem Centraal wants fewer parking spaces in the city center only when public transport, cycling, and peripheral parkings are well arranged. This sequence is necessary to keep the city accessible and livable.

109383.explanation: Arnhem Centraal primarily wants more social housing where the shortage is large, aiming for at least 35% social rental housing. In neighborhoods with already a lot of social housing, we opt for a better mix to keep neighborhoods mixed and livable and reduce the gap between rich and poor.

109384.explanation: Housing is important, but not at the expense of nature and living space. Arnhem Centraal chooses to build first within the city and convert vacant (office) buildings. Our greenery is what makes Arnhem unique and must be protected.

109385.explanation: The livability of the city center is under pressure. Therefore, (unfortunately) unpleasant measures are necessary. The transition for small businesses with temporary exemption must be realistic and flexible. After all, sole proprietorships are very important for the diversity of our city center.

109325.explanation: The Party for the Animals strives for an equal society. Political representation remains skewed, also in Arnhem. It is important as a municipality to be aware of this and strive for equal representation.

109326.explanation: Many residents want to have a say in their city or neighborhood and their involvement is increasingly important. In our opinion, the advisory referendum should be given a role in major decisions such as large construction projects or municipal reorganization. We also make neighborhood referenda possible.

109329.explanation: Art and culture form an indispensable basis for a life in freedom, so the nightlife culture in Arnhem must also have space. In granting permits, the surroundings must be well considered, ensuring that the peace of residents and nearby nature is preserved.

109331.explanation: Arnhem remains a place where all people, regardless of residence status or papers, have the right to dignified treatment, safe housing, protection against premature or unlawful expulsions, and against immigration policies that violate human rights.

109333.explanation: Arnhem must completely ban wood burning, both indoors and outdoors. Wood smoke causes tremendous fine particulate pollution and health problems for many residents. Arnhem will provide a reporting point for burning nuisance and inform residents about the disadvantages of wood burning.

109335.explanation: Fireworks cause a lot of nuisance for people, animals, nature, and the environment. We advocate festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers, provided that people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer harm.

109337.explanation: Both animals and nearby residents can experience significant disturbance from festivals and large events. Therefore, the municipality sets strict rules to counter noise nuisance and light pollution.

109338.explanation: In an inclusive society, all residents must be able to participate; therefore, in Arnhem, there must be equal rights for people with disabilities. We ensure strict compliance with the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities adopted in 2016.

109342.explanation: Child development is important. Music is part of this. Therefore, the municipality invests in sports, swimming lessons, culture, music lessons, and (independent) nature and sustainability education in schools.

109343.explanation: Sports and exercise take an important place in our municipality. Sports promote our physical and mental health. Sports also have a social function, and sports clubs play an important role in this.

109345.explanation: The Party for the Animals chooses more street workers, neighborhood parents, and community police officers, the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. We also want more attention for crime prevention, for example by investing in youth in disadvantaged situations.

109346.explanation: Camera surveillance in public spaces must be used very cautiously for privacy reasons. Camera surveillance only offers false security and often merely shifts the problem elsewhere. Automated facial recognition is an excessive measure and should not be used.

109348.explanation: Providing loans is not a solution to the housing crisis and drives prices further up. The municipality must strongly focus on sufficient affordable homeownership and especially more social rental housing. A home should also be findable without all kinds of arrangements and loans.

109349.explanation: The municipality must ensure that enough social rental homes are available for everyone who needs them in Arnhem.

109354.explanation: The Participation Act, with its strong focus on paid work and its coercive nature, has failed. Arnhem must put the resident and their needs at the center with a broad and generous view on social assistance recipients. Delivering a counter-performance for social assistance does not belong in this vision.

109356.explanation: The municipality has the task of preventing poverty. Poverty and financial problems do not exist only among people with incomes at the social minimum. The income threshold for poverty assistance must remain raised to at least 130% of the social minimum.

109358.explanation: The introduction of a basic income creates more equality. The municipality will inform the national government, which decides on this, that Arnhem wants to conduct a pilot with a basic income.

109361.explanation: Providing and maintaining parking facilities costs a lot of money; it is fairer if the user of this facility also pays. This also encourages people to choose sustainable and healthy transport.

109363.explanation: Healthcare must take into account differences such as gender, culture, origin, or sexual orientation. We also do everything to stop discrimination in healthcare and special attention will be given to women's health.

109367.explanation: Tourism causes a lot of damage to the climate and nature due to associated polluting transport and overconsumption. Therefore, the municipality must use the proceeds of the tourist tax to make tourism more sustainable and strengthen nature to limit the damage from tourism.

109368.explanation: Space in Arnhem is scarce. Vacant lots and empty buildings must be used for housing construction. Other unused space is needed for full-fledged greenery and nature. As a result, there is no room for the expansion of industrial sites.

109370.explanation: Arnhem is a place where all people have the right to dignified treatment and safe housing.

109371.explanation: The polluter must pay. For most residents, we want the recycling center to remain free, and we maintain the option to have bulky waste collected for free a few times a year. However, people and companies with excessive amounts of waste should be required to pay.

109375.explanation: For a good distribution across the city, we want a coffeeshop in Arnhem South. We do not allow new coffeeshops near schools.

109379.explanation: Social cohesion among residents and neighborhood connections are important for livability, health, and safety. It is therefore important to invest in youth centers, community centers, district centers, and meeting places, with special attention to youth, the elderly, and people with disabilities.

109381.explanation: The Party for the Animals chooses to reclaim public space from the car. The municipality removes parking spaces in desired locations and transforms them into wider sidewalks, children's play areas, flower-rich verges, small parks, or bicycle parking spaces.

109383.explanation: Housing is a right, not a luxury! Therefore, more affordable homes must be built: at least 40% of rental homes should be social housing. However, not all neighborhoods can accommodate more social housing. Social rental homes should mainly be built in neighborhoods with a smaller share of social housing.

109384.explanation: No building in green areas! To keep Arnhem livable, we cherish existing greenery. For spatial developments, we want a broad exploration of nature values — for example through independent ecological research — that goes beyond the legal requirements.

109385.explanation: The Party for the Animals chooses clean transport to promote clean air. Polluting traffic is banned from the city center. A zero-emission zone will come into effect for commercial traffic in the center from June 1, 2026.

109325.explanation: We find it especially important that the aldermen come from Arnhem, that the number of aldermen is reduced, and that aldermen fall under an 80% salary scheme so that more money remains available for investing in society.

109326.explanation: The referendum is a wonderful addition to our representative democracy. It also increases the involvement and influence of certain groups, especially for those with practical education, making it an excellent tool to gain more influence on policy.

109329.explanation: This would place too heavy a burden on the enforcement capacity in Arnhem.

109331.explanation: Asylum reception in Arnhem can be reduced to the level required by the Distribution Act. Currently, Arnhem accommodates three times more than it should. That is too much.

109333.explanation: This form of paternalism is also unenforceable. Moreover, there is a bigger problem that needs to be addressed: the ongoing pollution caused by the biomass plant Kleefse Waard.

109335.explanation: We oppose a fireworks ban because then the good must suffer for the actions of the bad. As compensation, the municipality can organize a professional fireworks display.

109337.explanation: The noise standards are currently set too high; they can be lowered without diminishing the experience of visitors.

109338.explanation: This is a responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves, not of the taxpayers of Arnhem.

109342.explanation: It is important that every child, regardless of the parents’ financial situation, can be exposed to art and culture.

109343.explanation: Sports clubs are of great importance in our society and play a major social role. Therefore, they deserve our support when necessary.

109345.explanation: Safety is a prerequisite to feeling comfortable in our city. It is an important task, and more enforcement officers can contribute to this.

109346.explanation: In notoriously unsafe areas, camera surveillance can be helpful. For example, in the city center and specific locations known to be at risk.

109348.explanation: This causes even more money to flow into an already overheated financial bubble. This will only lead to further increases in housing prices.

109349.explanation: If people want to continue living in their own city, that is a logical wish the municipality should facilitate. This also preserves the much-needed social cohesion in certain neighborhoods.

109354.explanation: The required counter-performance leads to undesirable displacement. Regular jobs are filled by welfare recipients working without pay.

109356.explanation: The threshold is now too low. We all know that those just above the social minimum are very vulnerable and should receive municipal support.

109358.explanation: Working people are entitled to a bigger share of the 'pie.' That means social struggle with the labor unions for higher wages for workers and less profit-driven motives from capital. If everyone receives a basic income, we can forget about this social struggle.

109361.explanation: Life is already expensive enough for many people, for example groceries.

109363.explanation: Quality, not identity, of the care provider should be paramount.

109367.explanation: Tourists enjoy facilities paid for by Arnhem taxpayers; there should be something in return.

109370.explanation: The priority scheme for status holders must be adjusted. Not to attack status holders, but to give the administrators who caused the housing crisis a headache they finally need to solve. Because now those same administrators are playing status holders against each other.

109371.explanation: The current system, with a certain number of times per year that it is (almost) free, works well.

109375.explanation: Of course, this should be done in good consultation with the surroundings and not near, for example, schools.

109379.explanation: Vibrant neighborhood centers are essential for the livability and social cohesion of vital neighborhoods.

109381.explanation: There is no surplus of parking spaces.

109383.explanation: More social rental homes should be built in Arnhem’s richest neighborhoods. In neighborhoods where there are already many social rentals (Geitenkamp, Immerloo), no new (social rental) homes need to be added.

109384.explanation: Building in parks should be avoided. At the same time, in Arnhem-North, especially around Hoogkamp and Schaarsbergen, there are areas with little natural value where suitable housing can be added.

109385.explanation: This is necessary for the viability of small entrepreneurs and people who simply cannot afford to buy a state-of-the-art electric car.

109325.explanation: Administrators should be selected based on suitability and experience, not gender. Equal opportunities are essential, but fixed quotas are not a good solution.

109326.explanation: Important political decisions are often complex and cannot be captured by a simple yes/no question. Therefore, the CDA opts for involvement through citizen assemblies and good participation, so residents can genuinely contribute and policies improve in content.

109329.explanation: Cafes and restaurants should have the freedom to determine their opening hours. At the same time, the municipality must be able to intervene if safety or livability is at risk, with targeted and temporary measures in consultation with those involved.

109331.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers no longer have a right to reception and must return to their country of origin. Establishing and implementing asylum policy is the responsibility of the national government, and municipalities should not undermine this policy. This keeps the asylum system clear and credible.

109333.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain possible, with attention to health and livability. Nuisance must be prevented during unfavorable burning moments. Therefore, the CDA wants to actively promote the Burning Guide and focus on clean and safe burning installations.

109335.explanation: Because there is a fireworks ban in Arnhem, a municipal fireworks show is a good and safe alternative. This preserves the tradition while considering safety and livability. The CDA is open to multiple shows, for example in neighborhoods in cooperation with neighborhood associations.

109337.explanation: Noise standards have been tightened in recent years and provide sufficient protection. The CDA believes the focus should be on proper enforcement, clear agreements with organizers, and timely informing of nearby residents.

109338.explanation: Accessible buildings are the responsibility of entrepreneurs and established in laws and regulations. The CDA believes this should be paid from their own business budget, not with municipal subsidies. For new construction and redevelopment, clear agreements on accessibility are necessary.

109342.explanation: Music education is already part of the elementary school program. Extra music lessons outside school are primarily the responsibility of parents. For low-income families, the CDA finds targeted support through existing schemes appropriate so that every child can participate.

109343.explanation: Sports clubs are important, but financial problems are not automatically a municipal responsibility. The municipality's role lies in good and affordable sports facilities. The CDA chooses targeted support, like help for volunteers or sustainability measures, not direct financial aid.

109345.explanation: The CDA wants to increase the capacity of municipal enforcement officers and equalize it with comparable municipalities. More community enforcement officers in the neighborhoods ensure visible enforcement and thus strengthen safety and livability.

109346.explanation: The CDA sees camera surveillance as a supplementary tool against crime and vandalism. Expansion is appropriate where residents or entrepreneurs request it, such as in neighborhoods and industrial areas. Privacy is carefully considered, but safety remains the priority.

109348.explanation: A starter loan is not a core task of the municipality and does not solve the housing shortage. The CDA believes such purchase schemes should be arranged nationally and that municipalities should primarily focus on building sufficient affordable homes.

109349.explanation: Local ties may be considered in housing allocation to keep communities livable, as long as this is done fairly and within the rules, and with room for urgent cases.

109354.explanation: The CDA believes participation is fundamental: those who can contribute should do so according to their ability. Meaningful social involvement increases self-esteem, social cohesion, and employment opportunities. The municipality supports where necessary but always encourages active participation.

109356.explanation: Raising income limits is an expensive, generic measure that does not always reach the right people. The CDA prefers tailored solutions and simple arrangements to ensure aid reaches those who truly need it and that working remains rewarding.

109358.explanation: The CDA opposes a basic income because it discourages work and abandons the principle that everyone contributes according to their capacity. It is costly and does not specifically address poverty and unemployment. The CDA prefers activating policies that guide people towards work and social participation.

109361.explanation: Free parking at shopping centers ensures good accessibility and prevents customers from going to other locations. It also supports local shops and keeps neighborhood facilities accessible.

109363.explanation: The CDA values freedom of choice in healthcare so that people can receive appropriate care when available. Care must remain accessible, of good quality, and comply with applicable laws and regulations for care providers.

109367.explanation: Arnhem must remain attractive and welcoming to visitors. A lower tourist tax helps keep the city competitive and boosts tourism, which benefits local amenities and the city's vibrancy.

109368.explanation: The CDA prefers restructuring existing business parks over expansion. Smart use of space strengthens the economy without adding pressure on nature and the living environment.

109370.explanation: Due to the shortage of social housing, unlimited priority for status holders is unfair to other urgent housing seekers, such as young people, victims of domestic violence, and those with medical or social needs. Housing allocation must therefore be fair and balanced.

109371.explanation: Making the recycling center completely free could lead to more waste and higher costs. It is reasonable that those who produce waste also contribute to its processing because otherwise costs are covered via waste levies for all residents.

109375.explanation: The CDA opposes further expansion of coffeeshops in Arnhem because this normalizes drug use, lowers the threshold for use, and increases risks of addiction and health damage, especially among young people.

109379.explanation: Community centers are the heart of the neighborhood and strengthen meeting and social cohesion, which helps prevent loneliness and (care) problems at an early stage. They offer low-threshold support and bring residents, associations, and social organizations together.

109381.explanation: Parking pressure is already high in the center and there are too few parking spaces. Even fewer spots make the city less accessible for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

109383.explanation: In neighborhoods with a lot of social housing, such as Geitenkamp or Klarendal, adding more social housing is not always desirable. A one-sided housing stock hinders mobility, reduces the district's capacity, and increases social problems.

109384.explanation: Building in parks and protected nature is often legally impossible and undesirable. At the same time, housing development on the city's edges, where it can be done responsibly and with green preservation, is sometimes necessary to address the housing shortage.

109385.explanation: For many entrepreneurs, diesel or petrol vans are still indispensable. As long as sustainable alternatives and charging capacity are insufficiently available, the city center must remain accessible to them. The transition to cleaner transport must be realistic and phased, within European regulations.

109325.explanation: At Volt, we believe in equal representation of women and men. For example, we require alternating placement of men and women on the list. At Volt, we fully support diversity.

109326.explanation: At Volt, we are against referendums. We believe in involving residents early on to prevent the need for a referendum. We support citizens' assemblies.

109329.explanation: Cafés and restaurants may determine their own opening hours within the frameworks set by the General Local Regulation (APV).

109333.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves are harmful to humans, animals, and the environment.

109335.explanation: Volt supports a ban on private fireworks use and encourages one central fireworks show.

109337.explanation: Arnhem is not a city with much space to organize festivals or parties without causing nuisance to nearby residents. Rules regarding noise standards must at least be better enforced. We support preventing hearing damage.

109338.explanation: Accessibility is important and we are happy to contribute to this from the municipality.

109342.explanation: Music is a wonderful medium that helps express emotions. It is important to introduce children to this form of culture and give them room to develop themselves through music.

109346.explanation: More camera surveillance does not automatically mean more safety. We support prevention.

109348.explanation: With a starter's loan, people can borrow more and thus drive up housing market prices. We support solutions like building affordable homes.

109354.explanation: This is directly opposed to introducing a basic income. We are in favor of a basic income.

109356.explanation: In Arnhem, the income threshold is already 120-130% of the social minimum. There is no need to raise it.

109358.explanation: At Volt, we fully support a basic income.

109363.explanation: There is a shortage in healthcare. Unfortunately, we do not have the privilege to offer every resident a care provider of their choice.

109367.explanation: There is a cost associated with keeping Arnhem beautiful and accessible.

109371.explanation: Bringing waste to the recycling center helps with waste separation and reduces illegal dumping.

109383.explanation: Some neighborhoods already have a lot of social housing. Building social housing in all Arnhem neighborhoods is not obvious. We must aim for a good distribution across the city.

109384.explanation: Parks and nature are important parts of a healthy living environment. There are enough other places in Arnhem where new housing can be realized; we do not need to build on green spaces.

109325.explanation: Voters determine the coalition and city executive in Arnhem. We want diversity in the broadest sense, not an imposition that reduces diversity to just male/female. Such a requirement assumes everyone has the same candidate pool. Parties propose their own teams. We demand genuine representation.

109326.explanation: Residents must have direct influence on major decisions. Referendums can be allowed with clear rules and protection of fundamental rights.

109329.explanation: Fewer rules and less paternalism: the municipality simplifies procedures so entrepreneurs have more space.

109331.explanation: No one should end up on the street; even when applications are rejected, humane shelter and guidance are necessary.

109333.explanation: No generic ban on wood stoves. Address nuisance and health risks specifically with enforcement and tailored measures. We oppose unnecessary paternalism in citizens’ private domains that affects living enjoyment. No symbolic politics, but real action where problems occur.

109335.explanation: A central fireworks show can be safer and limit nuisance; preferably regulated and safe. DENK has previously voted in favor of a central fireworks show.

109337.explanation: Festivals are part of Arnhem; tackle nuisance specifically but do not impose unnecessarily stricter general standards.

109338.explanation: Arnhem must be accessible and inclusive, so that everyone can participate in public spaces and facilities.

109342.explanation: Extra attention and funding for workshops, music lessons, and arts programs in disadvantaged neighborhoods.

109343.explanation: Sports clubs deserve serious investment in facilities (fields, changing rooms, space).

109345.explanation: More visible neighborhood police officers and enough enforcement officers (boa’s) in the districts.

109346.explanation: No surveillance state: camera monitoring only when demonstrably necessary, with strict privacy and discrimination assessments.

109348.explanation: Help starters with an (interest-free) starter loan (possibly enhanced with halal mortgage principles) and targeted starter policies.

109349.explanation: Residents of Arnhem and the region get priority for 50% of the homes.

109354.explanation: Social assistance is tailored support; dignified governance and guidance to work, no punitive coercion.

109356.explanation: Increase minimum provisions so that more people qualify (including school expenses, laptops, sports/culture).

109358.explanation: Social security needs renewal; a basic income trial can be explored.

109361.explanation: Affordable parking: lower rates and no expansion of paid parking.

109363.explanation: Culturally sensitive care is a top priority; cultural sensitivity becomes a requirement in municipal tenders.

109367.explanation: Tourist tax can be lowered if entrepreneurs suffer and city tourism benefits.

109368.explanation: Strengthen the local economy: municipality invests in SMEs and procures more goods/services locally.

109370.explanation: Arnhem meets the target for status holders and guides newcomers from day one towards language, work, and participation.

109371.explanation: Waste policy must be fair: no Diftar that affects low and middle incomes; producers/polluters should be charged more.

109375.explanation: No expansion without clear justification; livability and safety come first.

109379.explanation: Strong neighborhood centers and meeting places in all districts so people continue to meet each other.

109381.explanation: Keep enough parking spaces for living, working, entrepreneurship, and informal care.

109383.explanation: More social and middle-rent housing is a top priority; affordable housing is central.

109384.explanation: Housing shortage sometimes requires difficult choices; if building on green space is truly necessary: careful consideration and strong compensation.

109385.explanation: City center remains accessible for commercial vehicles and delivery vans, even if they are not yet fully electric.

109325.explanation: Appearance or origin may never be selection criteria.

109329.explanation: Consideration must be given to the surroundings, so residents are not woken up in the middle of the night by disturbances.

109331.explanation: More than 3,000 status holders and asylum seekers already live in Arnhem. Enough is enough. No expansion and no replenishment after departure.

109335.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. If the municipality organizes it, we’ll see four civil servants busy with such matters again. There are already too many office staff.

109337.explanation: The surroundings must not experience nuisance. Thumping is not necessary. GelreDome generally manages bass levels well.

109343.explanation: The municipality can offer facilities but sponsorship is undesirable.

109345.explanation: The current number of 35 is absolutely too few. Citizen Interest Arnhem also wants enforcement officers to be directly reachable for Arnhem citizens via a separate phone number, not just via the police.

109346.explanation: Cameras can work preventively. In practice, camera surveillance proves its usefulness.

109348.explanation: Why provide rent subsidy but not help people who fall just short of supporting themselves? Pure discrimination!

109349.explanation: A status holder should NOT automatically get priority for housing. Status holders should join the queue just like Arnhem residents. A mother or father in a shelter with children is just as urgent as an asylum seeker or status holder.

109354.explanation: That would be nice. Unmotivated people, because they HAVE to, are often just a burden!

109356.explanation: Living in poverty is no joke.

109358.explanation: We oppose that. This should be arranged nationally. Otherwise, Arnhem will no longer be attractive only for status holders, but also for benefit claimants. Arnhem the dumping ground of Arnhem? We don’t think so!

109361.explanation: To win the competition, free is important.

109363.explanation: This must remain within financial frameworks. Care should be appropriate and fall within the DUTCH culture.

109367.explanation: No one will avoid Arnhem because of the tourist tax. In Austria, you also pay a few euros per night.

109368.explanation: Unfortunately, this left-wing college and majority of the council chose for fewer industrial sites. Ridiculous beyond words. BurgerBelang voted against it. Industrial sites provide employment.

109370.explanation: Certainly not. A status holder should NOT automatically be given priority for housing. Status holders should join the queue just like Arnhem residents. A mother or father in shelter with children is just as urgent as an asylum seeker or status holder.

109371.explanation: It prevents littering. Maybe you should even get money when you turn in certain items.

109375.explanation: Not more coffee shops, but better distribution. On the other hand, it's odd to ban advertising for meat, fish, and fashion while cheering a coffee shop that contributes to brain damage.

109379.explanation: Neighborhood centers have been systematically dismantled by the municipality over the past 20 years. To break loneliness and improve cohesion, a neighborhood center can be useful.

109381.explanation: If you want to get rid of the city center, it's a good idea. We need a vibrant city center and more parking spaces!

109383.explanation: Building should be done based on demand.

109384.explanation: Certainly not. Arnhem should expand towards Lingewaard (Elst and Heteren). So expand Schuytgraaf instead of empty meadows.

109385.explanation: There is also a ban on passenger cars from 1-1-2030. We must reverse that. Driving diesel or petrol is less harmful than fine dust from tires.

109325.explanation: Equal representation is an important goal, but not a strict requirement.

109326.explanation: We support good participation and prior consultation. An advisory referendum or survey can help, but ultimately the municipal council must be able to weigh all interests and make decisions.

109329.explanation: From a safety perspective, it is desirable that opening hours in an area are uniform, so that police or enforcement deployment can be coordinated accordingly. Livability and peace for residents are important. Rules help to limit nuisance.

109331.explanation: Even rejected asylum seekers are people. Basic shelter prevents inhumane situations and social problems.

109333.explanation: Wood burning can cause nuisance and health problems. We want stricter rules, but no total ban. In rural areas, rules may be more lenient than within built-up areas.

109335.explanation: A fireworks display increases safety and reduces nuisance, damage, and pollution, and fosters togetherness.

109337.explanation: Loud noise causes nuisance to residents and hearing damage to visitors. Events are valuable but must not come at the expense of the health and livability of residents and visitors.

109338.explanation: Accessibility is a basic right. Everyone must be able to participate independently. Entrepreneurs may receive support in this.

109342.explanation: Art and culture contribute to the broad development and creativity of children. The municipality must commit to making music lessons possible for every child, especially supporting those with lower incomes.

109343.explanation: Amateur sports clubs are important for meeting others, exercise, and health. Therefore, they deserve support from the municipality, for example in maintaining their facilities.

109345.explanation: We are concerned about nuisance in Arnhem and the limited capacity to enforce rules, as it creates a feeling of insecurity. Enforcement is needed for safety and livability in neighborhoods. Besides enforcement officers, we also consider deploying street corner workers.

109346.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance, with attention to privacy, can help address structural insecurity and nuisance. It is important that the quality of the footage is sufficient to take action.

109348.explanation: Starters need help to purchase their first home.

109349.explanation: We already have policies that give housing seekers from the region a better chance at social rental housing. But living in Arnhem should remain accessible to others as well.

109354.explanation: People's well-being is paramount; socially meaningful work can contribute to that, but coercion has the opposite effect.

109356.explanation: Poverty also affects working poor. We want to pay more attention to this group. By raising the income threshold (for part of the schemes), we soften the poverty trap when residents work more.

109358.explanation: We are open to experiments, but want a clear understanding of effects and costs first.

109361.explanation: This requires tailor-made solutions per location, considering affordability, accessibility, and livability.

109363.explanation: People should be able to receive care that fits their beliefs and background.

109367.explanation: Tourist tax helps finance facilities without increasing the burden on residents. We are open to a slight increase in the tourist tax in the coming years if needed.

109368.explanation: Economic development is important, but we currently see no need for extra space. We want to first fill vacancies on industrial sites.

109370.explanation: Status holders need housing quickly to integrate swiftly into our municipality.

109371.explanation: Separating and handing in waste must be easily accessible to prevent littering. This is currently allowed six times a year. We want to increase this to eight times.

109375.explanation: Drug use contributes to nuisance, addiction, health problems, and incurs high social costs.

109379.explanation: Neighborhood centers strengthen encounters, coexistence, and informal care.

109381.explanation: There is already high parking pressure on weekends. Fewer parking spaces will cause nuisance. Additionally, parking spaces generate revenue that helps keep rates low. The P+R at Gelredome is a good solution for cheap parking and visiting the center.

109383.explanation: We support an undivided city and mixed neighborhoods. Some neighborhoods need more (social) owner-occupied homes (like Geitenkamp), while others need many more social rental homes.

109384.explanation: The housing shortage requires choices, respecting green spaces and nature. We advocate adding green neighborhoods to the edges of the city.

109385.explanation: Arnhem's center must remain accessible to residents and entrepreneurs, and not everyone has an electric car.

109325.explanation: So-called background characteristics should not be part of a selection process; that does not fit liberal universalism.

109326.explanation: Certainly. Democratic steering and/or guidance through referenda is very valuable; preferably following the Swiss model.

109329.explanation: Initially yes, though certain reasonable limits might be negotiable.

109331.explanation: A city government is primarily tasked with caring for its own residents, so Arnhem should first provide housing and support for its own inhabitants before worrying about others again.

109333.explanation: Burning wood should be a basic right. Education on efficient and low-smoke burning is permitted.

109335.explanation: No. A government should not organize a show at taxpayers' expense; let Arnhem businesses do this themselves, possibly with some municipal support.

109337.explanation: Not lower, but perhaps other sound absorption techniques.

109338.explanation: This is a private matter. A government should not concern itself with that.

109342.explanation: Music lessons are not a government service.

109345.explanation: Arnhem faces a major safety challenge. Arnhem has repeatedly asked for more officers but did not receive them - the only other option is then to increase municipal enforcement with public order officers (BOAs).

109346.explanation: Yes, but this camera surveillance may only be used to a limited extent, and no AI recognition software may be applied due to privacy protection.

109348.explanation: The costs of buying homes can be lowered in other ways.

109349.explanation: A municipality must take care of its own residents as the first priority.

109354.explanation: The word 'meaningful' is still difficult to define, but requiring a counter-performance is only reasonable.

109356.explanation: The municipality should actually reduce poverty assistance anyway; this has traditionally been a task often fulfilled by social institutions such as the Caritas Institute and other related charities.

109358.explanation: No. Those trials have already been conducted and appear to have little positive effect.

109361.explanation: Shopping centers are important places for our city; good accessibility is crucial.

109363.explanation: In principle, FVD supports the principle of freedom of choice - however, this is difficult in a time when many contracts are agreed upon long term.

109367.explanation: The tourism tax must be abolished. We should never financially penalize people in the Netherlands for visiting somewhere.

109368.explanation: If Arnhem wants to grow and create a better live-work environment, that will be necessary.

109370.explanation: Never. We believe that a government, state, or 'ruler' always has the primary duty to its own citizens. Once they are provided for, help for others can be considered cautiously. So no priority for newcomers.

109371.explanation: No. Nothing is free, but perhaps cheaper and a scheme where people may, for example, dump waste for free once a year - possibly turning it into an event.

109375.explanation: It would not be good if a government agency interferes with which companies may or may not establish themselves; that is up to the market. Only in clearly risky cases or where criminal connections are involved should a government intervene.

109379.explanation: No. Let a community center be a local initiative - only if an established community center dies out should a government consider providing support.

109381.explanation: No, quite the opposite. More parking spaces mean a greater chance of urban tourism.

109383.explanation: No. Social rental housing can have a negative effect on existing neighborhoods; it is better to build in new neighborhoods.

109384.explanation: If there is insufficient space to build within existing buildings, nature must give way to development.

109385.explanation: Absolutely. Not every company is capable of replacing their entire 'fleet,' as it is called, with electric buses. This will be a disaster if a zero-emission zone is established.

109325.explanation: There are fewer women in politics.

109354.explanation: Social assistance is not optional plus work experience.

109368.explanation: Good for the economy and jobs

109325.explanation: We prefer to see as many women as possible in politics. The executive board has a role model function, and women have a different perspective on matters, such as public space and balancing economic interests with wellbeing.

109326.explanation: LEF supports preferential voting with multiple options, not just a yes/no or for/against choice. The wording of questions can be very suggestive or even confusing.

109329.explanation: Cafés and restaurants can best judge which opening hours work for them. Additionally, this reduces nighttime nuisance as guests go home at more spread out times.

109331.explanation: LEF wants to provide shelter to those who need it. The alternative is that asylum seekers become homeless, which only hinders contact and support in returning to their home country.

109333.explanation: Wood stoves should be banned to improve overall air quality. LEF wants to explore the possibility of subsidies to replace wood stoves with green heating sources.

109335.explanation: A joint light spectacle on New Year's Eve should become the new tradition. Think of laser projections, a light show with drones, and music acts. We will stop using noisy fireworks that frighten pets, residents, and people with PTSD for days.

109337.explanation: You sometimes hear noise in the city center when standing outside but not when inside. Let people enjoy their parties. LEF is for fun (if you don’t want nuisance, there are plenty of quiet neighborhoods to live in). We love great parties. Who wouldn’t want that for us?

109338.explanation: Entrepreneurs may want to help but often don't know how. The municipality can organize informational afternoons or even challenge entrepreneurs to a physical role-play to experience what it is like to have a disability.

109342.explanation: Music education is good for brain development and developing skills that contribute to good school results. Besides that, learning music is also fun!

109343.explanation: Sports are extremely important to stay physically and mentally healthy. Being a member of a club increases the chance to meet people and find an (unpaid) job. It also saves healthcare costs, so it is a good investment!

109345.explanation: More enforcement officers do not address the core problem behind insecurity. LEF wants a safe city by investing in equal opportunities, neighborhoods where residents know each other, and sufficient social control on the streets. More street coaches is not a bad idea.

109346.explanation: Surveillance cameras and the underlying systems are expensive and do not necessarily provide more safety. They mainly provide visibility. Additionally, Arnhem residents have a right to privacy.

109348.explanation: Starter loans mainly benefit homeowners because they drive up house prices. Starters pay more in the long term due to the extra amount on their mortgage. LEF prefers that Arnhem invests in better housing flow, for example through a moving allowance for seniors.

109349.explanation: It is important that people who have a connection with Arnhem can continue living in the city. This creates more social cohesion and people who invest long term in their neighborhood and city.

109354.explanation: A reciprocal obligation helps people stay socially engaged. Volunteer work gives people purpose and dignity, keeping them involved in society.

109356.explanation: LEF supports basic security. More help must be provided for people with low incomes to give them the opportunity to improve their life situation where possible.

109358.explanation: Yes, a basic income promotes equality, independence, and social cohesion because all forms of work are rewarded. We want to start a pilot with basic income as soon as possible.

109361.explanation: LEF wants to make public transport as attractive as possible within Arnhem. For shopping centers, this is not always realistic because some purchases are transported by car. Therefore, LEF wants to make parking spots at shopping centers blue zone areas.

109363.explanation: People must be ideologically free and have a free choice in where and from whom they wish to receive care.

109367.explanation: We gladly welcome tourists to Arnhem, but this does mean extra maintenance for our city. Through the tourist tax, they contribute to this. LEF believes that the current tourist tax is affordable and does not need to be cheaper.

109368.explanation: There is unnecessary clutter in the landscape. Besides being unattractive, it wastes our valuable space. Existing business parks can be better upgraded. Project developers seeking profit on new locations should be prevented.

109370.explanation: People who have fled due to dire circumstances need a stable environment to build a life. Priority ensures that status holders integrate faster into the municipality and become valuable participants in our society.

109371.explanation: We want to encourage the disposal of bulky household waste. However, we want to explore possibilities to charge a fee for recycling where possible.

109375.explanation: Arnhem has enough coffeeshops. There is nothing wrong with smoking a joint, but we don't need to expand the supply.

109379.explanation: LEF supports slowing down and more meeting places. Community centers are places where people can get to know each other and social groups mix. We also want offline zones where people spend less time on their phones and meet in real life to build genuine connections.

109381.explanation: LEF wants fewer cars in the city center and full commitment to free public transport, Park & Ride locations, and shared transport. Cycling should be made more attractive by placing more bike racks.

109383.explanation: We want to maintain the number of social rental homes. Preferably, we will introduce the Housing Preferences App. Every resident fills in their housing wishes there. Do you like bustle or quiet? Do you want to live together or alone? How many rooms? A housing law will ensure that only what is needed is built.

109384.explanation: No, the parks and nature in Arnhem must be preserved. It is better to add living spaces vertically on existing houses.

109385.explanation: No, LEF supports clean air. That is not compatible with burning fossil fuels.

109325.explanation: Good point, men and women are equal according to the law.

109326.explanation: Arnhem decides for itself: introduction of a local, binding referendum. Residents must be able to directly participate in decisions on major projects such as new construction and closure of asylum seeker centers.

109329.explanation: No, it should be arranged by the municipality.

109331.explanation: Close asylum seeker centers. We are in favor of closing all asylum seeker centers in Arnhem, including luxury passenger ships at the Rhine quay. Our motto is: close the borders now! Shelter in the region!

109335.explanation: No fireworks ban: we firmly support traditional consumer fireworks (category F2 and F3). A popular festival belongs in the Netherlands.

109337.explanation: Yes, it should be clearly established how high the norm is so that others are not inconvenienced.

109342.explanation: Music is important for the development of a child.

109343.explanation: Playing sports is very important and healthy for everyone. Sports clubs that are struggling should be assisted by the municipality.

109345.explanation: More police, more supervision: investment in additional neighborhood officers on the streets and the establishment of a special police team for shops in the center, Kronenburg, and Presikhaaf. Deployment of high-quality 4K/8K camera surveillance at criminal hotspots.

109346.explanation: More camera surveillance in unsafe places, preferably a 4K or 8K camera system that is easy to review, so that this increases the detection and capture rate of perpetrators.

109348.explanation: Great plan to provide a starter loan.

109349.explanation: Dutch people have priority in obtaining rental housing. We will abolish the privileged position of status holders who immediately receive social rental housing. Dutch housing seekers must wait between 10 and 15 years, while status holders receive social rental housing within a few months.

109354.explanation: Even more, the municipality of Arnhem should require social assistance beneficiaries to accept at least 20 hours of work. This is within the framework of quid pro quo; the time of asking for help without giving anything in return is completely over.

109358.explanation: Complete nonsense. We are strongly opposed to basic income.

109361.explanation: Free parking in the city center for a maximum of 2 hours using a parking disc.

109368.explanation: That will bring new employment opportunities to Arnhem.

109370.explanation: Dutch people have priority in obtaining rental housing. We will abolish the privileged position of status holders who immediately receive social rental housing. Dutch housing seekers must wait between 10 and 15 years, while status holders receive social rental housing within a few months.

109371.explanation: Of course free, that increases the chance that people will return everything.

109375.explanation: Control over drugs: we support the municipal cannabis experiment with legal cultivation. The revenues (millions for Arnhem) will go to safety and healthcare. We concentrate coffee shops in the center, away from the neighborhoods.

109379.explanation: Good plan, a community center fosters connection in a neighborhood.

109381.explanation: Free parking in the city center for a maximum of 2 hours using a parking disc.

109383.explanation: Building for our own people: realization of affordable social rental and owner-occupied homes (40%/60%) in expansion areas towards Elst. No construction on the green meadows of Beukenlaan in Geitenkamp.

109384.explanation: To solve the housing shortage, we will have to see if we can build in parks or nature areas.

109385.explanation: Realistic environmental policy: no expansion of the environmental zone. However, research into hydrogen buses for non-electrified lines. All municipal vehicles will switch to hydrogen.

statements.title: Arnhem's centre should remain accessible to vans that run on diesel or petrol.
The residents of Arnhem should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Arnhem moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Arnhem should continue to provide shelter to rejected asylum seekers.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Arnhem.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Arnhem should run a basic income pilot.

titleParts.text: Arnhem moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Arnhem should hire more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: More social rental homes should be built in all Arnhem neighbourhoods.

titleParts.text: In alle Arnhemse wijken moeten meer

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Arnhem.

titleParts.text: In Arnhem moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: There should be fewer parking spaces in the centre.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to live in the Netherlands. The term can mean that right itself, but also the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Parking at shopping centers must remain free.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: Business parks in Arnhem should be allowed to expand.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality should allow building in parks or natural areas.
